notes: Current phase is team_profile only; no score, group, champion, or betting output.
fan_summary_en: Mexico under Aguirre look less like a romantic possession project and more like a high-pressure host team built on midfield bite, wide energy, and crowd-fueled momentum swings.

sample_summary.primary_window: 2026 pre-World-Cup preparation plus official squad context
notes: Local team packet is still shallow, so this deepening pass uses official Mexico and FIFA material plus the existing team packet.
Tactical micro-details remain partial until a larger structured match sample is available.

coach_profile.summary: Aguirre's Mexico is built less around stylistic purity and more around competitive edge, emotional control, and usable game-state flexibility. The host context matters: Mexico are being prepared to start fast, absorb pressure, and ride momentum without losing their midfield bite.

team_shape.in_possession_shape: Back four base with aggressive fullback support, one holding midfielder protecting central rest-defense, and a front line that can mix direct striker reference play with wide carries.
team_shape.out_of_possession_shape: Often a 4-1-4-1 or compact 4-4-2 pressing shell depending on whether the second forward joins the first line.
role_asymmetry: Mexico can alternate between Santiago Gimenez and Raul Jimenez as the central reference depending on matchup and game plan.
Wide roles are used to create crossing volume, diagonal runs, and crowd-driven momentum rather than endless sterile possession.

key_roles.note: Primary balance piece, ball-winner, and emotional tone-setter. Mexico's defensive floor and pressing bite are closely tied to him.
Gimenez offers more running and future-facing vertical pressure; Raul offers link play, hold-up value, and composure. Mexico's attack profile shifts depending on which version Aguirre prefers.
Still a key wide accelerator and crowd-energy amplifier when available and trusted for major minutes.
Important for defensive line authority, left-sided coverage, and keeping the back line from becoming too stretched.
A key part of match control when Mexico need stability in high-emotion phases.

attack.summary: Mexico's attack is built around wide attacks, fullback support, and using the center-forward as either a runner or a wall-pass reference. They can create strong momentum bursts, but pure chance conversion remains a live question.
notes: Host energy and emotional surges can push Mexico into high-volume attacking phases.
The striker profile choice changes whether Mexico look more vertical or more connective in the final third.
Set pieces and crowd-driven pressure can raise short-run shot volume.

defense.summary: Mexico defend best when the central screen stays intact and the team can press without exposing the space behind aggressive fullback behavior. Their best version is intense but not reckless.
notes: If the press is mistimed, the space behind the line can become a real issue.
Edson Alvarez is central to restoring order after emotional or chaotic stretches.
The host setting may amplify both confidence and volatility.

set_pieces.attack: Set pieces are a meaningful support weapon, especially in a home environment where momentum can stack quickly.
set_pieces.defense: Defensive set-piece floor is acceptable but still needs a fuller map of takers, markers, and late-game lineups.

coach_tendencies.leading_adjustments: Aguirre is likely to protect the middle first, retain competitive edge, and manage emotion rather than chase extra spectacle once ahead.
coach_tendencies.trailing_adjustments: When trailing, Mexico can add another attacking reference, increase crossing volume, and push the emotional tempo higher.
coach_tendencies.summary: Aguirre's reputation and current messaging point to a pragmatic tournament coach who values resilience and game-state control over stylistic vanity.

attack_ceiling.note: Home energy, wide attacks, and striker flexibility raise Mexico's ceiling, especially in momentum-heavy matches.

defensive_floor.note: The floor is acceptable when Alvarez anchors the structure, but fullback exposure keeps it from grading higher.

set_piece_attack.note: Useful secondary route, especially in emotionally charged home settings.

tempo_preference.note: Mexico can deliberately raise tempo and pressure phases rather than staying at one rhythm.

transition_attack.note: Transitions are strongest when the striker and wide runners sync well.

transition_defense_risk.note: This remains a real concern when fullbacks push and the first line is bypassed.

key_player_dependency.note: Alvarez is the balance hinge, while striker selection and wide form shape the attacking version Mexico becomes.

market_relevant_flags.note: His presence strongly affects Mexico's midfield control and defensive stability.
This choice changes the final-third profile from more vertical to more connective.
The more aggressively Mexico push from fullback, the more both chance volume and transition exposure rise.
Mexico's home context can magnify positive momentum as well as volatility.

verification_questions.claim: Mexico's deepest tournament version is a pragmatic 4-3-3 or 4-2-3-1 built on Alvarez and wide pressure.
verification_questions.why_challengeable: Needs more full-match evidence across strong-opponent samples.

verification_questions.claim: Striker selection materially changes the attack profile rather than only changing the name on the team sheet.
Fullback space behind the press is one of Mexico's main structural risks.
verification_questions.why_challengeable: Needs video-backed rest-defense review and a wider match sample.

verification_questions.claim: Home context meaningfully raises Mexico's attacking momentum and pressure capacity.
verification_questions.why_challengeable: The emotional effect is real but difficult to isolate cleanly from opponent quality.

data.completion_note: Deep version completed to a usable handoff level, but packet remains partial pending a fuller structured sample, set-piece map, and official T-75m lineup context.
